turns.content: Abeg how I fit send money to my sister for Port Harcourt? She dey use Access Bank.
You fit use your banking app or USSD code. Which bank you dey use? If na Access too, e go fast well well.
I dey use GTBank. She go collect am today?
If you send am before 2pm, e go enter her account today. GTBank to Access Bank, e no suppose pass 2 hours during business hours. The charges na around ₦50-100 depending on the amount.
Okay. But if I use OPay or PalmPay, the charges dey different?

Yes o. Opay and PalmPay dey cheaper — sometimes free transfer sef. But your sister go need the app too if you want send am to her wallet. If na straight to bank account, e still go work but the charges fit small.
Abeg give me the USSD code for GTBank make I try am quick.
Na *737# — dial am, select 'Transfer', then put Access Bank, her account number, and the amount. E go ask for your PIN. Make sure say you get enough balance.
I don dey feel headache and body pain since yesterday. E serious o. Where I fit go see doctor for Ikeja wey no go cost too much?

Sorry o. For Ikeja, you fit try the general hospital for Alausa — na government hospital so e no dey expensive. Or private clinic like First City Hospital for Allen Avenue. How the pain dey? E come with fever?
Yes, small fever dey. And my neck dey pain me join.

Ah, that one fit be malaria symptoms o. You don do test before? I go advise make you go do malaria test first before you start to take any medicine. General Hospital charges like ₦2,000-₦3,000 for the test.
I never do test. But my friend say make I just buy Lonart for pharmacy, e go clear am.

No o! No just dey take malaria medicine without test. E fit no be malaria — e fit be typhoid, or just stress, or something else. Make you go do test first. And if na malaria, the doctor go tell you the correct dosage. Lonart wey you take anyhow fit cause resistance.
Okay I go go General Hospital tomorrow morning. Wetin time dem dey open?
I dey go CMS from Yaba. Which bus I fit take wey go quick pass?

From Yaba to CMS, your best bet na BRT bus. Dem get dedicated lane so traffic no go hold you. You go enter Yaba BRT park, take the one wey dey go CMS/Marina. E suppose take like 40-50 minutes depending on the queue.
How much be the BRT now? I get my Cowry Card.

From Yaba to CMS na around ₦300-₦400 with Cowry Card. Make sure say your card get balance o — dem no dey collect cash for BRT again. If your card no get money, you fit top am for the park before you enter.
Wetin happen if I no get Cowry Card? I just collect the card last week and I never activate am.

You fit activate am for any BRT park — just tell the attendant say you wan activate your card. But if you no get card at all, you fit buy one for ₦500 and dem go activate am immediately. Without card, you no fit enter BRT o. The alternative na danfo (yellow bus) wey go cost like ₦200-₦300 but dem suppose slow well well for Third Mainland traffic.
Make I just activate my card for Yaba park then. Thanks!
I wan order jollof rice and chicken for my office for Victoria Island. Which restaurant dey deliver fast for that side?

For VI, you get plenty options. You fit use Chowdeck or Glovo app — dem get plenty restaurants wey dey deliver. The Chicken Republic for VI dey fast, or you fit try The Place restaurant for Adeola Odeku. Wetin be your budget?
Budget no too matter, I just want better jollof. And the chicken must be fresh o. No be that one wey dem cook since morning.

I feel you! For proper jollof with fresh chicken, I go recommend 'The Yellow Plate' or 'Bukka Hut' for VI. Dem dey cook fresh every time and dem get good reviews. Delivery from Chowdeck go take like 30-45 minutes depending on traffic.
Okay make I try The Yellow Plate. But how I go know say the food still hot when e reach?

Chowdeck get 'hot delivery' guarantee. If your food cold when e reach, you fit report am and dem go refund or send another one. Also, you fit put 'please ensure food is hot' for the delivery instruction. The Yellow Plate dey pack their food well well with foil and insulated bag.
Nice one. Abeg add pepper soup join — I dey crave am since.
My laptop just dey show blue screen anyhow since yesterday. I don restart am like 5 times. Wetin fit cause am and how I fit solve am?
Wahala! Blue screen fit come from plenty things — bad driver update, overheating, or corrupt system file. Which Windows you dey use? And e dey show any error code when the blue screen come?
Na Windows 11. The error talk say 'DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE' — I no even understand wetin that one mean.

That error mean say one of your drivers dey misbehave when the laptop dey try sleep or wake up. E fit be graphics driver or WiFi driver. You fit try update your drivers? Open Device Manager, check which one get yellow warning sign, then update am. Or better still, go to your laptop brand website (Dell, HP, Lenovo) and download the latest drivers from there.
I use HP. But the thing be say I no fit even stay for the desktop for long before the blue screen go come again.

Ah, that one tough small. Try boot am for Safe Mode — as soon as you turn on the laptop, press F8 or Shift+F8 repeatedly. Safe Mode go load only basic drivers so the blue screen no go show. Once you dey inside Safe Mode, you fit update your drivers or even do system restore to the last time wey e dey work fine.
Okay make I try this Safe Mode thing. If e no work, I fit carry am go computer village?

Yes, if Safe Mode no solve am, Computer Village for Ikeja na your best bet. But be careful o — follow person wey you trust. Tell the technician the exact error code 'DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE' so dem no go tell you say na motherboard problem when na just driver update you need. E suppose cost around ₦10,000-₦15,000 maximum for that kind fix.
